response:
The differential equation models satisfaction growth where the rate depends on the current level and the gap to the maximum.
Actions speak louder than words
***
Substituting the proposed solution into the left side yields a derivative that matches the right side perfectly after simplification.
Actions speak louder than words
***
Solving for the constant C using the initial condition S0 reveals that C equals the natural log of the ratio of M to S0.
***
To find the time T when satisfaction reaches three quarters of M, we substitute this specific value into our derived formula for S(t).
***
Algebraic manipulation of the logarithmic terms allows us to isolate the variable T on one side of the equation completely.
***
The final expression for T depends directly on the constant k and the logarithm of the ratio between the initial state and the target state.
